content.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  annotations:
    deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: "1"
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"apps/v1","kind":"Deployment","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"auth-checker","namespace":"elasticsearch"},"spec":{"replicas":1,"selector":{"matchLabels":{"app":"auth-checker"}},"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"auth-checker"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["/bin/sh","-c","sleep infinity\n"],"env":[{"name":"ELASTIC_PASSWORD","valueFrom":{"secretKeyRef":{"key":"password","name":"elastic-password"}}}],"image":"curlimages/curl:8.5.0","name":"checker","readinessProbe":{"exec":{"command":["/bin/sh","-c","curl -s --max-time 5 -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} \\\n  http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate \\\n  | grep -q '\"username\":\"elastic\"'\n"]},"failureThreshold":6,"initialDelaySeconds":10,"periodSeconds":5,"timeoutSeconds":2}}]}}}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-07-03T09:56:49Z"
  generation: 1
  name: auth-checker
  namespace: elasticsearch
  resourceVersion: "1999989"
  uid: 3cce3d52-7126-4a6b-8fca-2cb015f9a2d4
spec:
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
  replicas: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: auth-checker
  strategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 25%
      maxUnavailable: 25%
    type: RollingUpdate
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: auth-checker
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - /bin/sh
        - -c
        - |
          sleep infinity
        env:
        - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              key: password
              name: elastic-password
        image: curlimages/curl:8.5.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: checker
        readinessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - /bin/sh
            - -c
            - |
              curl -s --max-time 5 -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} \
                http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate \
                | grep -q '"username":"elastic"'
          failureThreshold: 6
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 2
        resources: {}
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30

content.thinking:
So:
- Current password: `elasticpass`
- New password: `elasticpass-elastic-password-next`

The `auth-checker` deployment:
- Gets the password from the `elastic-password` secret
- Uses it to authenticate against Elasticsearch at `http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate`

I need to:
1. Change the elastic user's password in Elasticsearch to the new password
2. Update the `elastic-password` secret to contain the new password
3. Restart the auth-checker pod so it picks up the new secret value
Let me first verify that Elasticsearch is running and accessible.

input.command: kubectl exec -n elasticsearch curl-test -- curl -s --max-time 10 -u elastic:elasticpass -k https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate 2>&1

content.thinking:
The Elasticsearch is running with HTTPS. The auth-checker deployment uses `http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate`, but it should be `https://`. Wait, but that's how the auth-checker is configured. Let me re-read the auth-checker probe...

Actually, the auth-checker uses `-k` flag? No, looking at the deployment YAML again:
```
curl -s --max-time 5 -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} \
  http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate \
  | grep -q '"username":"elastic"'
```

It's using HTTP instead of HTTPS, which explains the exit code 52 error—the server's rejecting the unencrypted connection. The real issue is that I need to rotate the elastic user password and get the auth-checker probe working, so I should focus on fixing the protocol mismatch in the curl command. I'm wondering if the auth-checker probe is using the wrong URL scheme—it's trying HTTP when Elasticsearch requires HTTPS. Let me check if there's an HTTP endpoint available.

content.text: The cluster uses HTTPS. Let me check if the auth-checker probe can work — it uses `http://` but ES requires HTTPS:
